summary: The 7 USER_FLOWS form a clean closed lifecycle (configure → execute → verify → score/cull/fuse/mutate → observe → replay → stop) and the Phase 6 REQ tables map well to it: every must-ship REQ I checked carries a real Related Flow, and the explicit-PRD citations I spot-checked (REQ-F-003 serialized traits, REQ-F-004 ~20 agenomes, REQ-NF-001 caps, REQ-I-004 bandit/RL) are accurate quotes. Most of the PRD's minimum-shippable-cut, demo list, and team table are covered. However, walking the PRD's "Hard parts / risks" section surfaces a CRITICAL gap: the two explicit anti-reward-hacking mitigations that the PRD calls the defense of "the core bet" — (1) HOLD JUDGES OUT OF THE BREEDING LOOP and (2) ROTATE CRITIC AGENOMES so the target keeps moving — have NO covering REQ-* anywhere (REQ-S-002 only forbids critics mutating content; rotation/held-out-judge is unmentioned). Two more PRD must-haves are only partially covered: the demo payoff "we EXECUTE the proposed transfer LIVE" (no flow covers running the executable check live on a room prompt as a demo step) and the de-risked gen-0 "Rule of Cool" authored seed agenome (no REQ states gen-0 is a real authored genome). Direction-A classical-ML credit assignment / value model beyond novelty scoring is also thin. Several genuine unknowns (held-out rubric, scoring weights, problem domains) are correctly bucketed open and flagged as question-for-human.

findings.finding: The PRD's two explicit anti-reward-hacking mitigations — 'hold out judges from the breeding loop' and 'rotate critic agenomes so the target keeps moving' — have NO covering REQ-* in any Phase 6 table. These are the PRD's stated defense of 'the core bet' (a weak critic council means evolution optimizes for fooling critics, not good ideas). REQ-S-002 only forbids critics from mutating candidate content/lineage/score policy; it does not require judge rotation or holding a judge out of the breeding/scoring loop. A grep for 'rotat' returns zero hits across all artifacts; 'held-out' hits only refer to the rubric/evidence, not to isolating judges from the loop the agents optimize against.
findings.evidence: PRD p.2 'Hard parts / risks — Fitness without ground truth (the core bet): ... Mitigation: ground critics in retrieval + executable checks, hold out judges from the breeding loop, and rotate critic agenomes so the target keeps moving.' vs REQUIREMENTS.md Verifier/Security tables (REQ-S-001..S-004, REQ-F-006) — no rotation/held-out-judge requirement; USER_FLOWS.md 'Verify Candidate Ideas' has no rotation step.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a must-ship functional REQ, e.g. REQ-F-016 'The verifier council shall support a held-out/rotating judge set isolated from the breeding loop, so the fitness target is not directly optimizable by evolving agents (MVP: a fixed held-out final judge plus configurable critic-set rotation across generations).' Add a corresponding step to the 'Verify Candidate Ideas' or 'Score, Cull, Fuse, And Mutate' flow and a security constraint that the held-out judge config cannot be mutated by agents.

findings.finding: The live-demo payoff step 'for transfer prompts — we EXECUTE the proposed transfer live' is only partially covered. REQ-F-007 and REQ-E-003 cover stored/replayable subtype-check evidence, and 'Verify Candidate Ideas' runs checks during the generation, but no REQ or flow covers running the executable transfer check LIVE on the winning idea as a demo moment (distinct from checks run inside the generation loop or shown via replay).
findings.evidence: PRD p.2 'What we'd demo ... (4) Payoff: ... for transfer prompts — we execute the proposed transfer live.' vs REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-E-003 (replayable evidence) and REQ-F-007 (checks during verification); USER_FLOWS.md 'Observe Live Run' / 'Replay A Run' have no live-execute-final-transfer step.
findings.proposed_fix: Either extend REQ-E-003/REQ-F-007 acceptance to include 'final surviving transfer idea's executable check can be re-run live during the demo', or add a demo-flow step in 'Observe Live Run'. If live execution is too risky for the showcase, explicitly mark the live-execute as replay-backed and note the deferral.

findings.finding: The PRD's de-risked starting point — the gen-0 'Rule of Cool' authored seed agenome ('frozen scaffold of exactly one Doppl agenome' that already works) — is not named by any REQ. REQ-F-003 says 'Seed and child agenomes persist trait fields' but does not require that generation-0 be a real authored/proven agenome rather than randomly initialized traits. This is load-bearing for the demo because the whole pitch is 'taking that proven scaffold, mutating it into a population.'
findings.evidence: PRD p.1 'De-risked starting agenome (we already have the seed) ... Rule of Cool ... the single-agent, human-authored, frozen scaffold of exactly one Doppl agenome.' vs REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-F-003 / REQ-F-004 (population spawning) — no requirement pinning gen-0 to an authored seed genome.
findings.proposed_fix: Add an acceptance bullet to REQ-F-003 or a new REQ: 'Generation 0 shall be seeded from a human-authored baseline agenome (the Rule of Cool scaffold), not random initialization' so later-gen-beats-earlier-gen is measured against a credible baseline.

findings.finding: Direction-A classical-ML beyond novelty scoring is thin. The PRD names 'learn an embedding of idea space', a 'learned value model to predict which agenomes are worth feeding', and 'credit assignment'. REQ-I-003 covers embeddings-for-novelty and REQ-I-004 covers heuristic (non-learned) allocation, but 'credit assignment' and a value model are not separately tracked, even as explicit deferrals.
findings.evidence: PRD p.1 'Classical ML governs the ecosystem (Direction A) ... we learn an embedding of idea space ... use a learned value model to predict which agenomes are worth feeding ... classical ML supplies control, credit assignment, and search.' vs REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-I-003/REQ-I-004 only.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a one-line deferral REQ (e.g. REQ-DEF-010) covering 'learned value model + formal credit assignment' as stretch, and confirm REQ-I-003's embedding is reused as the idea-space embedding the PRD names (note the dual use).

findings.finding: REQ-F-008 lists 'novelty/diversity signals if present' as a fitness component conditionally, but REQ-I-003 makes simple novelty scoring must-ship MVP and EVALUATION_CRITERIA promotes it to firm MVP scope. The 'if present' hedge in REQ-F-008 is weaker than the locked decision and should be tightened.
findings.evidence: REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-F-008 'novelty/diversity signals if present' vs REQ-I-003 'must-ship' and EVALUATION_CRITERIA.md 'Simple novelty scoring is now MVP scope'.
findings.proposed_fix: Edit REQ-F-008 to require a novelty component (with a documented fallback only if embeddings are unavailable, matching REQ-I-003's acceptance) rather than 'if present'.

findings.finding: The held-out idea-quality rubric — the literal acceptance proof ('gen N+1 measurably beats gen N on a held-out rubric') — is still open, as are the scoring weights across critic/check/novelty/energy components. Without these, REQ-E-001, REQ-F-008, and the demo's central claim cannot be objectively verified.
findings.evidence: REQUIREMENTS.md lines 190-192 (open questions) and EVALUATION_CRITERIA.md line 32 '[open question] The exact held-out rubric is not yet defined.'
findings.proposed_fix: Decision needed: lock a concrete v1 rubric + component weights before the event/scoring model hardens. Recommended default: a 5-axis 0-5 LLM-judge rubric (grounding, novelty, feasibility, falsification-survival, subtype-check pass) with equal weights minus a small energy-efficiency tiebreak, judged by a held-out model not used in the breeding loop; refine after provider research.

findings.finding: Three flow-level open questions remain unresolved and gate the demo script: live-room prompt vs prepared vs both; which exact problem sets are available; and streaming-vs-polling level. These affect REQ-O-001 (rehearsable set) and REQ-F-015 (SSE) acceptance.
findings.proposed_fix: Decision needed before rehearsal. Recommended default: offer BOTH a room prompt and a prepared problem set with operator choice (REQ-O-001 already requires the prepared set); use SSE streaming for live with persisted-event polling as fallback (matches REQ-F-015); curate 2-3 vetted transfer/zeitgeist problem sets with a known-good replay.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Single-generation loop on a fixed problem set (minimum shippable cut)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Bounded run from seed/fixed set (F-001), prepared problem set (O-001), at least one successor generation (F-011).

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Spawn ~20 agenomes (min cut)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Explicit citation 'spawn ~20 agenomes' is accurate; bounded population within caps.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Reproduce by Fusion — crossover + output-level synthesis (min cut)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Two-level fusion explicitly cited; child records carry parent IDs + fusion metadata.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Run the critic council (min cut)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Structured critic reviews with distinct mandates; PRD mandate list matches.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Cull, mutate, re-run surviving lineages (min cut)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Cull/parent-select (F-009); successor generation (F-011); mutation in 'Score,Cull,Fuse,Mutate' flow step 6.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Show generation N+1 measurably beats generation N on a held-out rubric (min cut / acceptance)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Improvement display (E-001) and successor gen (F-011) covered, but the held-out rubric itself is an unresolved open question (REQUIREMENTS.md L190) — acceptance not yet objectively verifiable.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Verification = critic council + one objective check per domain, executable transfer scored pass/fail (min cut)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Critic council (F-006) + subtype-specific checks with skip records (F-007); 'executable cross-domain transfer check, pass/fail' cited accurately.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Instrumented dashboard: population tree, energy per agent, fitness over generations (min cut)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Dashboard live state incl. lineage/energy/scores (F-013); energy scarcity visible (E-004); legible breed story (UX-001).

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Agent genome = serialized {system-prompt + persona/value-weights + tool permissions + decomposition policy + spawn budget}
prd_req_coverage.notes: Citation verified verbatim against PRD p.1 Technical Approach.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Metabolism: finite energy; reasoning/tools/spawning all cost energy (compute scarcity = environment)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Hard caps (NF-001), no cap bypass (S-001), energy visible as selection pressure (E-004); USER_FLOWS 'Execute Generation Lifecycle' records energy spend.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Classical ML governs ecosystem (Direction A): bandit/RL spawn allocation
prd_req_coverage.req_id: out-of-scope (learned allocation deferred; heuristic required for MVP per REQ-I-004)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Matches LOCKED deferral; REQ-I-004 keeps MVP heuristic allocation, learned bandit/RL stretch. Correctly handled.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Learn an embedding of idea space + novelty/quality-diversity scoring
prd_req_coverage.notes: Simple novelty via embeddings is MVP (I-003); DPP/MAP-Elites quality-diversity correctly deferred (REQ-DEF-006). But the 'idea-space embedding' as a reusable model and learned value model/credit assignment are not separately named (see nice-to-have finding).

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Learned value model + credit assignment (Direction A)
prd_req_coverage.req_id: out-of-scope (learned value model not tracked; allocation deferred via REQ-I-004)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Neither a value model nor 'credit assignment' is named even as an explicit deferral; should be added as a one-line deferral REQ for completeness.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Critic council is itself the fitness function (argue-with-yourself council)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Critic reviews feed decomposed fitness (F-008); council-as-judge captured.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Hard rail: metric mutations survive only if they keep correlating with bedrock anchors (objective can evolve, anchor cannot move)
prd_req_coverage.notes: USER_FLOWS L195 'Metric mutation cannot redefine bedrock validation anchors' and L148 'held-out anchors cannot be moved' capture the rail, but no Phase 6 REQ pins it; if metric mutation is deferred for MVP it should say so explicitly.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Hard rail: ground critics in retrieval + executable checks
prd_req_coverage.notes: Retrieval/search grounding researched (I-002), executable subtype checks (F-007), no arbitrary code exec (S-003).

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Hard rail: hold out judges from the breeding loop
prd_req_coverage.notes: CRITICAL — no REQ requires isolating the judge/rubric from the loop agents optimize against. The PRD's core-bet defense. See critical finding.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Hard rail: rotate critic agenomes so the target keeps moving (anti-reward-hacking)
prd_req_coverage.notes: CRITICAL — 'rotat' appears nowhere in artifacts. REQ-S-002 only forbids critics mutating content, not the rotation mechanism. See critical finding.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Anti-mode-collapse: explicit novelty pressure + Fusion across distant lineages
prd_req_coverage.notes: Novelty pressure MVP (I-003); but 'Fusion across DISTANT lineages as an anti-collapse force' is not specified in REQ-F-010 — fusion parent-distance selection policy is unstated.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Cost & termination: hard energy caps, depth limits, kill the runaway tree (safety rail we demo)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Caps (NF-001), hard stop/kill path (O-003), operator stop preserving evidence (F-012), cap-enforcement tests (T-002). Load-bearing invariant satisfied.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Two-week realism: self-evolving verifier + in-house fine-tuning explicitly stretch, not MVP
prd_req_coverage.notes: Correctly deferred matching LOCKED list.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Demo (1) Seed: hand the organism a live unsolved prompt from the room
prd_req_coverage.notes: Run-from-seed (F-001) supported; whether the showcase uses a live room prompt vs prepared is an open flow question (USER_FLOWS L343).

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Demo (2) Watch it live: spawn, spend energy, critics, weak lineages go dark, strong pairs fuse + mutate
prd_req_coverage.notes: Live dashboard (F-013), legible spawn/survival/fusion/mutation story (UX-001), SSE streaming (F-015).

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Demo (3) Generations climb: fitness chart rises, lineages visibly specialize
prd_req_coverage.notes: Gen-over-gen improvement (E-001); visible lineage specialization promoted to MVP (E-002).

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Demo (4) Payoff: surface best surviving idea, replay the adversarial gauntlet
prd_req_coverage.notes: Final-idea inspection + critic gauntlet (UX-003, E-003).

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Demo (4) Payoff: for transfer prompts, EXECUTE the proposed transfer LIVE
prd_req_coverage.notes: Stored/replayable check evidence covered, but no REQ/flow covers live re-execution of the winning idea's transfer check as a demo moment. See important finding.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Demo contrast: generational — losers' useful fragments spliced back, round N+1 genuinely smarter (not a one-round tournament)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Fusion recombines parent traits (F-010) and gen-over-gen improvement shown (E-001).

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: De-risked starting agenome: gen-0 is a real human-authored 'Rule of Cool' scaffold (one working agenome)
prd_req_coverage.notes: F-003 persists seed agenome traits but does not require gen-0 be an authored/proven baseline vs random init. See important finding.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Recruiting hook: it's a kernel, not a SaaS wrapper around existing APIs
prd_req_coverage.req_id: out-of-scope (architectural posture, locked baseline — not a testable product REQ)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Captured as LOCKED 'custom TS kernel = authoritative runtime' and PRODUCT_BRIEF 'not a SaaS pipeline'; not a Phase 6 requirement, correctly.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Team / surface ownership: kernel/runtime, selection/ML, verifier council, demo/observability (3-4 engineers)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Modular ownership for the four surfaces; PRD team table cited accurately.

prd_req_coverage.prd_must_have: Both prey: cross-domain transfer + zeitgeist synthesis (one lifecycle, subtype-specific checks)
prd_req_coverage.notes: Both subtypes (F-002), subtype-specific checks (F-007), subtype+evidence preserved (D-004). Matches LOCKED canonical CandidateIdea unit.
